title: Bridge Essay: Painful metaphors: enactivism and art in qualitative research.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Painful metaphors: enactivism and art in qualitative research.**. Med Humanit.. PMID: 33077504. DOI: 10.1136/medhum-2020-011874. Enactivism is an emerging theory for sense-making (cognition) with increasing applications to research and medicine. Enactivists reject the idea that sense-making is simply in the head or can be reduced to neural processes. Instead, enactivists argue that cognisers (people) are embodied and action-oriented, and that sense-making emerges from relational processes distributed across the brain-body-environment. We start this paper with an overview of arecently proposed enactive approach to pain. W
